description:
If the auditd daemon is configured to use the augenrules program to read audit rules during daemon startup (the default), add the following lines to a file with suffix .rules in the directory /etc/audit/rules.d: 
-w /var/spool/cron -p wa -k cronjobs
If the auditd daemon is configured to use the auditctl utility to read audit rules during daemon startup, add the following lines to /etc/audit/audit.rules: 
-w /var/spool/cron -p wa -k cronjobs
rationale:
In addition to auditing new user and group accounts, these watches
will alert the system administrator(s) to any modifications. Any unexpected
users, groups, or modifications should be investigated for legitimacy.
ocil_clause: command does not return a line, or the line is commented out
ocil:
Verify Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 generates audit records for all events that affect "/var/spool/cron" with the following command:

$ sudo auditctl -l | grep /var/spool/cron

-w /var/spool/cron -p wa -k cronjobs
fixtext:
Configure Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 to generate audit records for all account creations, modifications, disabling, and termination events that <tt>"/var/spool/cron"</tt>.
Add or update the following file system rule to <tt>"/etc/audit/rules.d/audit.rules"</tt>:
-w /var/spool/cron -p wa -k cronjobs

The audit daemon must be restarted for the changes to take effect.
srg_requirement: Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 must generate audit records for all account creations, modifications, disabling, and termination events that affect /var/spool/cron.
title: Ensure auditd Collects Changes to Cron Jobs - /var/spool/cron
